

# News


## European Conference on Quality in Official Statistics 2026

We will be at the European Conference on Quality in Official Statistics (Q2026), which will take place from 3 to 5 June 2026 in Šibenik, Croatia. 

- We will have a dedicated session on [*Collaborating With Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning for Quality*](https://www.q2026.hr/programme/conference-programme/session-21/){target="_blank"}, with presentations from Eimear Crowley (WP1, Coordination), Olivier Meslin (WP3, AI/ML Lab), Luis Sanguiao (WP5, and MLUtils), and Remco Paulussen (WP7).
- Luis Ferreira will be presenting in [Session 3](https://www.q2026.hr/programme/conference-programme/session-3/){target="_blank"} on *Unlocking Legacy Statistics with Large Language Models*, based on work carried out by WP12.
- In the [Speed Talk Session 3](https://www.q2026.hr/programme/conference-programme/speed-talk-session-3/){target="_blank"}, there will be a number of presentations from members of AIML4OS, including Alexander Kowarik, Johannes Gussenbauer, Elena Rosa-Perez and Pedro Sousa
- In [Session 8](https://www.q2026.hr/programme/conference-programme/session-8/){target="_blank"}, Sónia Quaresma will present on the work of the Ecosystem Monitoring workpackage (WP4), with a talk titled *No one is without knowledge except he who asks no questions*. Klaudia Peszat will also present in this session with a talk on *The usefulness of good old ML models in real estate statistics*. 
- In [Session 13](https://www.q2026.hr/programme/conference-programme/session-13/){target="_blank"}, Nina Niederhametner who works with WP10 will present on *Using AI for Automatic Data Extraction and Classification in Official Statistics*. Giulio Massacci will also present in this session with a talk titled: *Who Works in the Green Transition? A Patent-Based Analysis of Occupational Change Using Large Language Models*. The session also includes Luis Sanguiao again, speaking on *High frequency time series imputation with auxiliary data: a deep learning approach*.
- There will be two speakers from WP5 in [Session 16](https://www.q2026.hr/programme/conference-programme/session-16/){target="_blank"}: Florian Dumpert will speak on *From the CoP to a quality concept for AI/ML in official statistics and back*, and Marco Puts has a talk entitled *The methodological view: current developments of the Total Machine Learning Error Model*.
- [Session 31](https://www.q2026.hr/programme/conference-programme/session-31/){target="_blank"} will have two presentations from WP10. Ariane Lestrade will speak on *Landscape of Text Classification with Machine Learning in Official Statistics: Experiences from the AIML4OS Project*, and Ewelina Niewiadomska has a presentation on *Using ML and LLMs for NACE classification*.





## Newsletters

- <a href="https://cros.ec.europa.eu/system/files/2025-03/1.NEWSLETTER%20AIML4OS.pdf" target="_blank">Our first newsletter</a>, highlighting the project launch, upcoming datalab presentation, and planned participation at NTTS 2025.
- <a href="https://cros.ec.europa.eu/system/files/2024-12/2.NEWSLETTER%20AIML4OS.pdf" target="_blank">The second newsletter</a> has info on three in-person meetings that took place, as well as more details on individual workpackages.
- <a href="https://cros.ec.europa.eu/system/files/2025-05/3.NEWSLETTER%20AIML4OS.pdf" target="_blank">Our third newsletter</a> was released after the first year of the project. It looked at plans across all of the workpackages, as well as an update following NTTS 2025.
- <a href="https://cros.ec.europa.eu/system/files/2025-07/4.NEWSLETTER%20AIML4OS.pdf" target="_blank">Newsletter number four</a> reports on an in-person meeting of WP12, more details on WP10, as well as a template for training materials.
- <a href="https://cros.ec.europa.eu/system/files/2025-11/5.NEWSLETTER%20AIML4OS.pdf" target="_blank">Our fifth newsletter</a> has details on a deliverable on citizen-in-the-loop, news on text classification, and progress on reconstruction of supply-chain networks.
- <a href="/resources/newsletters/newsletter_6.pdf" target="_blank">The sixth newsletter</a> has details on a deliverable on citizen-in-the-loop, news on text classification, and progress on reconstruction of supply-chain networks.



<br>

<div style="width:100%; max-width: 600px;" >
<iframe src="https://www.linkedin.com/embed/feed/update/urn:li:ugcPost:7452985254119653376?collapsed=1" 
height="567" 
width="100%" 
frameborder="0" allowfullscreen="" title="Embedded post">
</iframe>
</div>

<div style="width:100%; max-width: 600px;" >
<iframe src="https://www.linkedin.com/embed/feed/update/urn:li:share:7445479879292325888?collapsed=1" 
height="567" 
width=100% 
frameborder="0" allowfullscreen="" title="Embedded post">
</iframe>
</div>



<div style="width:100%; max-width: 600px;" >
<iframe src="https://www.linkedin.com/embed/feed/update/urn:li:ugcPost:7393689435994996736?collapsed=1" 
height="567" 
width=100% 
frameborder="0" allowfullscreen="" title="Embedded post">
</iframe>
</div>



<div style="width:100%; max-width: 600px;" >
<iframe src="https://www.linkedin.com/embed/feed/update/urn:li:share:7380985427874328577?collapsed=1" 
height="567" 
width=100% 
frameborder="0" allowfullscreen="" title="Embedded post">
</iframe>
</div>
